items.title: 'They simply lied': Iranians devastated as US signs with regime they hoped would fall

children.content: From inside Iran, citizens say they feel abandoned and forgotten as the United States signs a deal with the regime Washington launched a war against.

gallery.altText: A woman in burqa walks on a street in Tehran infront of wall with IRGC planes on it.
People walking on a road in Tehran

thumbnail.altText: A woman in burqa walks on a street in Tehran infront of wall with IRGC planes on it.
People walking on a road in Tehran

items.title: From a White House podium, Vance delivers 'stunning development' on Israel

children.content: The US vice-president takes public aim at Israel with a series of comments Washington watchers say are "unprecedented".

image.altText: JD Vance holding press conference.

items.title: Former NSW premier 'unhappy with progress of whistleblower claims'

children.content: Mike Baird tells the parliamentary hearing he was not happy with the pace of investigating whistleblower claims in the KPMG scandal. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A bald man in a suit and tie purses his lips.

items.title: Treasurer labels reports he has frosty relationship with PM as 'trash'

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers pushes back on reports of a rift, saying they've talked almost every day since the budget was handed down.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers in a grey suit

items.title: Pauline Hanson says businesses 'can't sack' lazy workers. What's the reality?

children.content: Pauline Hanson's claims that Australian businesses are "tied" to lazy workers they "can't sack", are labelled inaccurate by some employment law experts and small business advocates, while one HR representative says businesses are "terrified to terminate".

image.altText: Pauline Hanson looks to the side seriously.

items.title: Burnham poised to challenge UK PM Starmer after 'circus' by-election win

children.content: Andy Burnham, a British Labour lifer, is expected to challenge Sir Keir Starmer for the country's top job after re-entering the House of Commons via a by-election.

gallery.altText: A man smiling and pointing, in front of a large crowd of people holding signs in support of him.

thumbnail.altText: A man smiling and pointing, in front of a large crowd of people holding signs in support of him.

items.title: US accused of trying to 'edit out' climate change in Antarctic report

children.content: The US has been criticised over its reluctance to refer to "climate change" at a major Antarctic meeting, with France describing it as a "dangerous precedent" that risks undermining science.

image.altText: Melting Antarctic sea ice drifts between large patches of ice.

items.title: Wife of AFL legend Tony Modra thanks first responders 'who saved his life'

children.content: The wife of AFL icon Tony Modra has expressed her gratitude to emergency services and hospital staff as the Crows legend remains in hospital in a "critical but stable condition" following a truck crash in South Australia.

image.altText: GettyImages-2236218521

items.title: 'Blue-sky thinking': North Queensland reacts to Rinehart's idea of giving islands to Musk

children.content: The Townsville Enterprise chief executive says "all ideas are welcome" after mining magnate Gina Rinehart suggested islands off North Queensland's coast should be offered to SpaceX CEO Elon Musk for satellite launches.

gallery.altText: A beach surrounding by hills and pure white sand swirling into the ocean

items.title: Albanese government finds itself in policy quicksand on three core issues

children.content: The government's announced changes to its tax package are seeking to allay the fierce post-budget backlash from vested interests and some commentators.

items.title: Live facial recognition cameras to be used by WA Police in Australian first

children.content: WA Police are set to become the first in the country to trial live facial recognition of crowds, describing the technology as safer than standard CCTV cameras.

image.altText: Police Commissioner is speaking to the press in front of a surveillance van.

items.title: Fluke, or are the Socceroos actually good enough to win against the US?

children.content: Commentators are writing off Australia in Saturday's early morning clash against the US.
Was the Socceroos' win against Türkiye a fluke or was the way the game went part of coach Tony Popovic's plan?
We take a closer look at whether Australia is being judged on the wrong metrics and why this team has fans excited.

image.altText: A snarling Patrick Beach with his gloved hand held in a tight fist. Text: A guide to the Socceroos and their play style

items.title: Obama centre opens with star-studded crowd but no Trump

children.content: All living US ex-presidents, with the notable absence of Donald Trump, have joined a star-studded line-up for the opening of Barack Obama's presidential centre in Chicago.

items.title: The pros and cons of retiring overseas with your pension

children.content: With some foreign countries offering cheaper living expenses, it is no surprise that moving overseas to retire is increasingly attractive to some older Australians. But before you up stumps and book that flight, read this.

children.content: Today's news quiz is heating up like El Niño. Will you strut like an internet-famous duck or get schooled like Prince George?

items.title: Government says possum bridges successful, but sceptics question data

children.content: The WA government has released night-vision images showing scores of native animals are using rope bridges and underpasses along a $1.46 billion highway in the South West region, but not everyone is convinced.

gallery.altText: A night vision image of two ringtail possums crawling across a rope bridge toward the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A night vision image of two ringtail possums crawling across a rope bridge toward the camera.

items.title: So-called ISIS bride believes terrorist group 'sucks', lawyer says

children.content: Police fear a so-called ISIS bride who recently returned to Australia will spread extremist ideology if she is freed on bail, but her lawyer says that will not be the case.

image.altText: A woman wearing a brown hijab sits in court.

children.content: A Salmon Tasmania advertisement promoted across Facebook and in cinemas has been removed following a breach in advertising standards.

items.title: New data shines light on state of EV public charger network

children.content: It's often claimed Australia has an EV public charger shortage, but analysis of exclusive data shows most fast charger sites are not congested.

image.altText: EV charging site with empty bays.

items.title: 'Sleepwalking into a catastrophe': Proposed data centre raises water fears

children.content: To its supporters, this proposed data centre will provide much-needed regional jobs, but in the nation's driest state, critics say the draw on river water to cool the machines could be too great.

image.altText: A brightly coloured model of a data centre.

items.title: Photographers 'ruining' personal moments during World Cup anthems

children.content: Photographers have been allowed to get very close to the coaches during national anthems at the World Cup, but not everyone is happy.

items.title: AFL matriarch helped Freeman's wife get to Australia, stored family's guns

children.content: Kay Reid, the mother of premiership players Ben and Sam Reid, reveals that she and her husband Bruce's deep ties to police killer Dezi Freeman and his family date back two decades.

image.altText: Screengrab from footage of Dezi Freeman wearing sunglasses and gesturing while speaking with police.

items.title: Sandilands says billionaires 'come out of the woodwork' ahead of new show

children.content: Radio shock jock Kyle Sandilands reveals the details of his next move after accepting a "piddly little" settlement of $12 million in his contract dispute with ARN Media.

image.altText: Kyle Sandilands speaks into a gold-coloured microphone in a radio studio.

items.title: More soft plastics needed as Australia's recycling capacity booms

children.content: Australia's soft plastics recycling industry is operating at nearly triple the capacity it was at now-collapsed national recycler REDcycle's peak, but consumers are yet to be convinced.

image.altText: A close up of shredded recycled soft plastic.

items.title: Start of NSW snow season missing key ingredient

children.content: The Snowy Mountains are not quite living up to their name as a slow start to the ski season leaves some seasonal workers out of a job.

image.altText: Images of patches of snow and grass atop mountain.

items.title: NT government extends town camp alcohol bans

children.content: The NT government is extending alcohol bans in town camps beyond 2027, in a move welcomed by the health sector as a win for community safety.

image.altText: A row of empty liquor bottles lined up on a kitchen bench, the adjoining walls are covered in marks and dents.

items.title: Lal Lal Falls runs dry as upstream 'greed' sees water vanish

items.title: Inquest to probe Adelaide fashion icon's death after using magic mushrooms

children.content: The death of an Adelaide fashion industry icon who suffered a cardiac arrest after taking magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment will be probed at a coronial inquest.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt is photographed outdoors with soft sunlight and greenery in the background.

items.title: Third person charged over Melbourne synagogue fire

children.content: Police have charged a 20-year-old from Melbourne's north-west with a number of charges including arson over a fire that destroyed the Adass Israel Synagogue in 2024.

image.altText: Firefights walk past fire damage and smashed glass at the Synagogue.

items.title: Alice Springs' beloved beanie festival celebrates weird and wonderful knits

children.content: As the Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates its 30th and final year, we're exploring the weird and wonderful handmade creations — and the characters working hard behind the scenes — that have made the annual event so special.

gallery.altText: A man smiling at the camera, on his head is a handmade beanie featuring a knitted monkey holding a banana.
A woman wearing reading glasses and a grey beanie, pink and purple hair rollers are attached to her knitted hat.
A man giving two thumbs up as he smiles at the camera, he is wearing a pointing handmade hat on his head.
